reviews.review_title: Not good. Keys don’t line up.
reviews.review_description: Recalibrate it over and over it still doesn’t work it’s goes for multiple keys instead of one I had one set up where I got it better I have to press it so many times to go to the next note.
Absolutely horrible.  Proper keys do not respond, after several calibrations, it plays more keys than what I am actually playing on piano which keeps it from working at all!

I love this app till I tried to play a song. I can’t line up my 88 keyboard . I adjusted the keys to 0 with ￼no luck then up 1 # at a time no luck . So with the keys out of line there’s just no sense . 
   Mybe an update to calibrate the keys by pressing all the C keys one at a time. My keys look like this . C D E   F  G ￼A B  . So when you line up the center of the piano with the keys . Not one C key is in the right spot. PLEASE FIX THIS !

I was excited to try this app.  I was able to pretty much line up my keyboard but it took awhile.  Also you have to re-align every time you take off the headset,   Plugged in a midi keyboard to the USBC port of the quest and that didn’t work.  So I have not been able to use this at all.  Hope there is an answer soon or I’ll have to ask for a refund.  Great idea but half baked.if it gets fixed I’ll quickly review again.

With MIDI cable, it works really nice, and the piano keyboard setup is very straightforward and easy.
What I really love, is that you can learn to improvise on jazz songs! Really nice!
I bought this game and it never loaded. It would restart my oculus right after I tried to open the game. This game did not let me play worst money spent ever.
I can't get started it won't recognise the highest key ..im using a 88 key korg lianno

reviews.review_title: Where is the rest of the tutorial?
reviews.review_description:
This can be a bit finnikey with the finger tracking but generally works well.
Only the 1st 3 lessons in the tutorial though and then it gets a bit hard to navigate.
Please update as it is the best out there at the moment not locked behind a subscription
Can detect keys pressed on your physical piano, just not accurately, which makes it useless to learn piano with it.

I was extremely disappointed after installing the app. It quickly recognized my Recital Pro keyboard, and setting up the virtual keyboard was easy. I could play and hear all the notes, which was a positive. However, when I started the first lesson, it only recognized the first C note and would not accept any other C notes across the keys. Because of this issue, I couldn't continue with the lesson, and the app wouldn't unlock the subsequent lessons.

When I opened the other lesson folder, there was no option to proceed. It simply displayed all the note chords available for practice, with no start button or any visual indication of how to begin the lesson. It felt like a useless app at that point. 

Additionally, I couldn't find a way to turn off the background sounds in the virtual environments. It was very distracting to hear other music and people mumbling while I was trying to focus on the lesson plan. I prefer immersion over passthrough, so this was quite frustrating.

I'm hesitant to go through a bunch of hoops on Discord and then wait forever for a response.  Rebooting my Quest 3 headset did not solve the problems . . . sadly refunding.

Well engineered and thought out! As a beginner, I'm very excited at how well this works with our regular piano, which had been idle for too long. Makes learning much more interesting and challenging. I'm looking forward to how this progresses, particularly with the beginner lessons. I'm hoping to see right hand and left hand exercises added soon. So well done -- I would gladly pay for it!

Connected my MIDI keyboard to my Windows laptop and installed the companion app and the relevant Yamaha MIDI drivers for my keyboard. Worked right away. Smooth user experience and very easy to use. I tried the other piano options as well (Virtual Piano and Real Piano) and they worked equally well, but the MIDI option was my favorite. Highly recommended.
Amazing work the developer is doing here, i`ve tried all the piano apps on quest2 and this one blows them all away and its free

Experienced amateur pianist here. Instant sense of how a song fits on the keyboard, how it sounds, and when I hit a wrong note. Better than sheet music. Metronome/Beatsaber-like experience is both fun and discourages bad habits like slowing down on hard parts or playing the note wrong over and over because you misread the sheet. This is the ONLY way to sight-read music. This app is awesome as it is, but I have suggestions.

Turn on note names and fingering labels and autoplay. Turn off the input stopper thing. This should be the default settings.

Note recognition didn't work with my real piano at all. I detected wrong notes myself by ear when my real piano didn't sound like the midi. 

A mode with working note recognition (audio only) and hides the handtracking hands (unless doing menu gestures) so I see my real hands would be AMAZING. In fact, if I could run this game menu with my controllers that mode would be fine. Handracking is a hindrance if it isn't working perfect, the option should be available shut that junk off. Maybe one day it will work well enough to depend on.

A shared library of uploaded songs (within the headset app) would reduce the need for us all to duplicate effort of song creation. Just browse and download, or upload to share after making something new and awesome. Simple rating system for contributions.

A full-mp3 audio experience would be great too. Like Beatsaber. Instead of hearing a midi when you play well, you could hear a real recording of a pro on a concert grand, or you could hear the duet part and the drums or whatever. The more real-music and game-ified this is, the more compelling it will be.

First, thank you developers for this VR piano, it is joyful to learn and to play. 🤟🤩✌️.

I don't play piano, but with this app I am going to enjoy playing and learning at the same time.  Please continue to add more lessons, more options.  This is a wonderful piano app for beginners to enjoy. Thank you.

The potential for learning piano through AR is huge, and this app takes it in a really exciting direction. The interface is super intuitive, and with my keyboard connected through a MIDI cable the notes were recognized very quickly and accurately. I love the piano roll map of the song's form, and the chord trainer game that gives you a chord and you can play it in any inversion. I had some trouble calibrating the keyboard but once I got it it was fine.

I have minimal piano training, and within minutes I was playing pieces I never thought I would have the time or the training to play.  This is the future of music education.  I cannot wait to see more instruments adapt this technology in creative ways.

reviews.review_title: Very good app with huge potential
reviews.review_description: Magic Keys is an extraordinarily helpful tool for learning new pieces of music or even for getting better with the piano in general. At the moment, the app contains everything necessary to use the app, but there is still enormous potential that will hopefully be exploited over time.

Such and awesome idea, loved using it with a real piano. Was able to make the needed adjustments to get the keys really ￼dialed in.

Wish you guys all the best!
Keep up the good work.
I gotta tell you: the idea is incredible and it is astonishing how well everything works already. I am currently learning to play the piano and this will be my new companion! 100% recommended.
Who knew I would absolutely love playing piano for hours. Now I actually can play nice music instead of horror notes

reviews.review_title: Lets you play above your level
reviews.review_description: Very impressive software, but sorely needs logic to skip or remap notes that are outside the range of the selected piano size.

Just found this. When I first got my quest (about 2-3 yrs-ago) I wondered if one could play piano in VR or with an actual piano (since hand tracking was a thing.) Discovered this and OH MY GOSH!!! This is epic! And it works a lot better than I would've expected out of a beta. Had a little trouble at first with uploading custom songs, but a quick look through of the "questions" channel inside the discord server, and a download from the website and I was all good. Thank you so much for making this!

We are using with a traditional piano a 73 key zender- with a re-jig it fits!
Realise its only funds that will bring more modern songs so everyone should give this a real push. its solid and will only get better. Myself and Wife and Son are all using it!
wish the developer all the best.!

This is a great app for all learning stages of piano.  Engaging, intuitive and fun.  I really like the overview presentation of the midi file and look forward to developments which incoporate the color selection Dom has made into the waterfall learn mode.
